tools.name: code_execute
tools.description: Execute JavaScript code in a sandboxed environment with no filesystem, network, or system access. Returns captured console output and the expression result. Use for data analysis, algorithmic computation, string manipulation, JSON transformation, statistical calculations, sorting/filtering arrays, or any task that benefits from deterministic code execution. Code runs with a 5-second timeout and 8 KB output limit. Available globals: Math, JSON, Array, Object, String, Number, Boolean, Date, RegExp, Map, Set, parseInt, parseFloat, console.log.

code.description: JavaScript code to execute. Use console.log() to produce output. The last expression value is also captured as the result. No require/import, no filesystem, no network access.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum execution time in milliseconds. Default 5000, max 10000.

useWhen: the user wants to run code or a script
the user needs data transformation, sorting, or filtering
the user wants algorithmic computation that goes beyond simple math
the task requires iterating over data, string manipulation, or JSON processing
a swarm agent needs to compute, analyze, or transform structured data
avoidWhen: simple arithmetic that math_solve handles directly
the user wants to run Python specifically (not supported — JavaScript only)
the task requires filesystem, network, or system access
tools.returnsSummaryShape: object with status (ok|error), output (array of console.log lines), result (last expression value as string), executionMs, truncated (boolean)

shortDescription: Execute JavaScript code in an isolated sandbox with no system access — for data analysis, algorithms, and computation

limitations: no filesystem, network, or system access

orchestratorUseCases.scenario: Swarm agent needs to analyze structured data returned by another tool
orchestratorUseCases.explanation: Agent searches for data, then uses code_execute to sort, filter, or compute statistics on the results

orchestratorUseCases.scenario: Agent needs to transform JSON data into a different structure
orchestratorUseCases.explanation: Agent writes JavaScript to restructure, merge, or pivot data
